title: Fix calendar view sizing in DashboardPage to be properly visible and well-fitted

requirements.summary: Resize and reformat the calendar view in DashboardPage so it fits properly within the screen without overflowing or being clipped, ensuring compact display and proper visibility on both mobile and desktop viewports while maintaining the app's terracotta/cream theme.
acceptanceCriteria: The calendar in DashboardPage is fully visible within the viewport on both mobile and desktop screen sizes.
No calendar cells, day headers, or navigation controls are clipped or overflow the screen.
The calendar appears compact and well-proportioned — not oversized or too spread out.
Day labels and date numbers are legible at appropriate font sizes.
The calendar container has a constrained max-width so it does not span the full page width unnecessarily.
Styling remains consistent with the app's terracotta (#C47A4A), dark-brown (#2A1A0E), and cream (#FAF5EE) color palette.

file_operations.description: Apply responsive sizing constraints to the calendar component by adding a constrained max-width wrapper (e.g., max-w-2xl or max-w-3xl) around the calendar section. Reduce font sizes for day headers and date numbers to compact values (e.g., text-xs or text-sm for headers, text-sm for date numbers). Adjust padding and margins within calendar cells to reduce overall calendar dimensions while maintaining legibility. Ensure the calendar container uses responsive padding (e.g., p-2 sm:p-4) and that all month navigation controls remain visible. Verify calendar grid styling uses appropriate gap spacing (e.g., gap-1 or gap-2) to keep cells compact. Test viewport fit on mobile (375px) and desktop (1280px+) breakpoints to confirm no overflow or clipping occurs. Preserve existing terracotta, dark-brown, and cream color classes throughout the calendar styling.
